active.title: writeMarker ignores atomicWriteFile boolean failure signal
active.detail: writeMarker() discards the boolean returned by atomicWriteFile (transaction-manager.ts:177). On a write failure the count is incremented and the timer runs while no marker file exists on disk. Impact is fail-open and self-healing (launcher may reap, the safe direction; 20s timer retries the write). atomicWriteFile already console.warn's on failure, so it is observable.

active.title: On-disk labels transiently lag the live holder set after a non-last end()
active.detail: end() decrements the in-memory count but does not re-serialize the marker, so on-disk labels keep an ended holder's label until the next write event. Explicitly documented and asserted as intentional in the unit test; the launcher reads only presence + TTL, never labels. Cosmetic/advisory metadata, no behavior impact.

refuted.title: Reference-count leak permanently shielding a wedged daemon
refuted.rationale: Hypothesized that a throw inside beginMaintenance's writeMarker after activeCount++ could pin the count and, with the always-on refresh timer, defeat the 180s-TTL reap mitigation. Refuted: atomicWriteFile (transaction-manager.ts:177-200) wraps all fs work in try/catch, returns false, and never throws; beginMaintenance's other ops are an integer increment and an array push. The throw path does not exist.
